name: Elm (إلم)
description: Saudi government-owned company providing digital transformation and e-services for government entities across KSA. One of the largest IT firms in the Kingdom.

name: STC Group
description: Saudi Arabia's largest telecom and digital services provider, offering cloud, cybersecurity, IoT, and enterprise IT solutions through its STC Solutions arm.

name: Solutions by STC
description: STC's ICT subsidiary providing managed IT, cloud computing, cybersecurity, and digital infrastructure solutions for enterprises in Saudi Arabia.

name: Takamol Holding
description: A holding company driving innovation and sustainable development across various sectors including HR tech platforms (e.g., Qiwa) in partnership with Saudi Ministry of Human Resources.

name: Tamara
description: Saudi Arabia's leading BNPL platform allowing customers to split purchases into up to 24 monthly installments with zero late fees. Fully Sharia-compliant, operating across thousands of merchants in KSA. Valued at over $1 Billion (Unicorn).

name: Tabby
description: Buy Now Pay Later platform enabling users to split any purchase into 4 interest-free monthly payments. One of the most downloaded financial apps in Saudi Arabia and the UAE. Achieved Unicorn status in 2023.

name: Foodics
description: All-in-one cloud-based restaurant management system and POS solution. Serves thousands of F&B businesses across Saudi Arabia and MENA. Raised $170M Series C funding in 2022.

name: Salla
description: Saudi Arabia's leading e-commerce platform enabling merchants to build and manage online stores. Powers tens of thousands of stores across the Kingdom with integrated payments, shipping, and marketing tools.

name: Al Rajhi Bank
description: One of the world's largest Islamic banks and Saudi Arabia's largest bank by assets, offering a full suite of Sharia-compliant personal, corporate, and investment banking services through an extensive branch and ATM network.

name: Saudi National Bank (SNB)
description: The largest bank in Saudi Arabia by assets, formed by the merger of NCB and Samba Financial Group in 2021. Provides comprehensive retail, corporate, and investment banking services across the Kingdom.

name: Riyad Bank
description: One of Saudi Arabia's oldest and largest banks, established in 1957. Offers personal, corporate, treasury, and investment banking services with a wide domestic branch network and international presence.

name: SAB – Saudi Awwal Bank
description: Formerly Saudi British Bank (SABB), SAB is a leading commercial bank offering personal banking (loans, mortgages, credit cards), corporate banking, and 24/7 digital account access. Strategic partner of HSBC.

name: STC Pay
description: STC Pay is one of Saudi Arabia's leading digital wallet and mobile payment services, operated by Saudi Telecom Company (STC). It allows users to send money, pay bills, and shop online. It became the first Saudi fintech unicorn valued at over $1 billion.

name: Tamara
description: Tamara is a leading BNPL platform in Saudi Arabia and the GCC, allowing customers to split purchases into up to 24 months with zero interest and no late fees. Fully Sharia-compliant, licensed by SAMA, and active at thousands of merchants across the Kingdom.

name: Tabby
description: Tabby is a major BNPL fintech operating strongly in Saudi Arabia, enabling shoppers to pay in installments or after delivery. Partners with major Saudi retailers including SHEIN, NOON, and Namshi. Valued at over $660M.

name: Moyasar
description: Moyasar is a Saudi-based payment gateway that connects businesses, banks, and institutions through a secure, integrated payments layer. Supports Mada, Visa, Mastercard, Apple Pay, and STC Pay. Fully licensed by SAMA.

name: Tawuniya – The Company for Cooperative Insurance
description: Saudi Arabia's largest and oldest insurance company, publicly listed on Tadawul. Offers health, motor, property, and life insurance products across the Kingdom.

name: Bupa Arabia for Cooperative Insurance
description: A leading health insurance provider in Saudi Arabia, joint venture between Bupa (UK) and Saudi shareholders. Specializes exclusively in health insurance and wellness programs.

name: Arabian Cooperative Insurance Group (ACIG)
description: Provides a wide range of cooperative insurance products including motor, health, property and liability insurance to individuals and businesses in Saudi Arabia.

name: Al Rajhi Company for Cooperative Insurance (Takaful)
description: Shariah-compliant insurance (Takaful) company affiliated with Al Rajhi Group. Offers motor, health, travel, home, and life takaful products across the Kingdom.

name: Saudi Pharmaceutical Industries & Medical Appliances Corporation (SPIMACO)
description: One of the largest and oldest pharmaceutical manufacturers in Saudi Arabia, producing a wide range of medicines including generics, OTC, and specialty drugs. Listed on the Saudi Stock Exchange (Tadawul).

name: Jamjoom Pharma Factory Co.
description: A leading Saudi pharmaceutical manufacturer specializing in sterile and non-sterile preparations including ophthalmic solutions, IV solutions, tablets, and ointments. Listed on Tadawul.

name: Riyadh Pharma Industries
description: Saudi pharma manufacturer producing generic drugs, nutritional products, and specialized formulations. Serves both domestic and regional markets.

name: National Unified Procurement Company for Medical Supplies (NUPCO)
description: Government-backed national procurement company responsible for centralizing the purchasing of pharmaceuticals, medical devices, and supplies for all Saudi Ministry of Health facilities.

name: National Unified Procurement Company (NUPCO)
description: Saudi Arabia's national platform for unified procurement of pharmaceuticals, medical supplies, and devices for government hospitals. Operates under the Ministry of Health and serves as the central procurement hub for the entire Kingdom's public healthcare sector.

name: Arabian Medical Company
description: A leading medical devices distribution and supply company in Saudi Arabia, providing hospitals and clinics with a wide range of diagnostic, surgical, and monitoring equipment.

name: Panda Retail Company
description: One of Saudi Arabia's largest supermarket chains, operating since 1978. A proud Saudi brand offering groceries, fresh produce, and household products across KSA.

name: Jarir Bookstore
description: Saudi Arabia's leading retail chain for books, computers, tablets, printers, smartphones, smart TVs, video games, and digital cards. HQ in Riyadh.

name: Tamimi Markets
description: A premium supermarket chain and the preferred choice for Saudis and expatriates, offering high-quality groceries, fresh foods, and international products.

name: SACO
description: Saudi Arabia's top home improvement retailer, offering tools, appliances, hardware, furniture, and home equipment both in-store and online.

name: Danube Supermarkets
description: A well-established Saudi grocery and supermarket chain under BinDawood Group, with branches across KSA offering fresh food, bakeries, and household goods.

name: KUDU Restaurants
description: Saudi Arabia's favorite homegrown restaurant chain since 1988. Serving made-to-order sandwiches, fresh salads, and meals across 350+ locations in the Kingdom. One of the largest QSR chains in KSA.

name: Al-Baik Food Systems Co.
description: Iconic Saudi fast-food chain founded in 1974 in Jeddah. Famous for its signature fried chicken and Broast. Operates 100+ branches mainly in the western region and expanding nationally. Cult-favorite brand.

name: Herfy Food Services Co.
description: Publicly listed Saudi fast-food and bakery chain (Tadawul: 6010). Operates 400+ branches across KSA offering burgers, broasted chicken, sandwiches, and bakery items. One of the largest F&B companies in Saudi Arabia.

name: Nando's Saudi Arabia
description: South African flame-grilled peri-peri chicken brand operating in Saudi Arabia with multiple branches in Riyadh, Jeddah & Khobar. Operated under local franchise. Known for its signature sauces and casual dining experience.

name: Abdul Latif Jameel Co. Ltd.
description: One of Saudi Arabia's largest automotive groups, operating for 80+ years across MENAT region. Official distributor of Toyota & Lexus.

name: Petromin Corporation
description: Leading multinational in KSA providing sustainable mobility solutions, automotive lubricants, fuel retail, and vehicle services.

name: Al Majdouie Motors
description: A major Saudi conglomerate with strong automotive division; authorized distributor of Volvo, UD Trucks, and Mitsubishi vehicles.

name: Mohamed Yousuf Naghi & Brothers (Motors)
description: Authorized distributor of BMW, MINI, and Rolls-Royce in Saudi Arabia. One of the most prestigious automotive groups in the Kingdom.

name: Saudi Arabian Military Industries (SAMI)
description: Saudi Arabia's national defense conglomerate, wholly owned by the Public Investment Fund (PIF). Umbrella company for all military-industrial production in KSA, targeting 50% domestic defense spending localization by 2030 under Vision 2030. Manages subsidiaries across land systems, aerospace, defense electronics, and weapons.

name: Saudi Space Agency (SSA)
description: The Saudi government authority responsible for developing and regulating the national space program. Oversees satellite launches, space exploration partnerships, astronaut programs, and coordinates KSA's strategic space ambitions as part of Vision 2030.

name: General Authority for Military Industries (GAMI)
description: Saudi Arabia's regulatory body for the military industries sector. Licenses and regulates domestic and foreign defense companies operating in KSA. Central to Vision 2030's goal of localizing 50% of military spending domestically by 2030.

name: Advanced Electronics Company (AEC)
description: A SAMI subsidiary and pioneer in defense electronics in Saudi Arabia. Specializes in radar systems, C4I systems, electronic warfare, communications, and avionics. One of KSA's most established defense technology manufacturers with 35+ years of experience.

name: Almarai Company
sub_sector: Dairy, Juices, Bakery, Poultry — Food & Beverage
description: The largest vertically integrated dairy food and beverage company in the world, producing dairy products, juices, bakery items, and poultry. Listed on Saudi Stock Exchange (Tadawul).

name: SADAFCO — Saudia Dairy & Foodstuff Company
sub_sector: Dairy, Tomato Paste, Frozen Food — Food & Beverage
description: A leading Saudi manufacturer and distributor of dairy, milk, cheese, tomato paste, and cream products since 1976. Operating under the iconic Saudia brand across the Middle East.

name: Tanmiah Food Company
description: A premium Saudi poultry producer raising cage-free, hormone-free, corn-fed chickens. One of the leading poultry brands in Saudi Arabia listed on Tadawul.

name: Sunbulah Group
sub_sector: Frozen Food, Dairy, Cheese — Food & Beverage

name: Saudi Aramco
description: The world's largest oil company and Saudi Arabia's national petroleum & natural gas company. Responsible for exploration, production, refining, distribution, shipping, and marketing of oil & gas.

name: SABIC
description: One of the world's largest petrochemical companies, producing chemicals, polymers, fertilizers, and metals. A subsidiary of Saudi Aramco.

name: Petro Rabigh
description: Joint venture between Saudi Aramco and Sumitomo Chemical. Operates an integrated refining and petrochemical complex along the Red Sea coast.

name: Tasnee — National Industrialization Company
description: Saudi Arabia's first fully privately owned industrial joint stock company. Active in petrochemicals, titanium sponge production, and industrial gases.

name: ACWA Power International
description: Saudi Arabia's largest developer, investor, and operator of power generation and water desalination plants. Leads major solar projects including the world-record Noor Abu Dhabi and Red Sea solar plants. Publicly listed on Tadawul (Saudi Stock Exchange).

name: Alfanar Company
description: One of Saudi Arabia's leading energy and electrical manufacturing companies. Alfanar Energy develops large-scale solar PV and wind energy projects across MENA, South Asia, and the UK.

name: NEOM Company
sub_sector: Renewable Energy / Smart City / Green Hydrogen
description: NEOM is a giga-project powered 100% by renewable energy. Hosts NEOM Green Hydrogen Company (NGHC) — a joint venture producing green hydrogen using solar & wind energy. THE LINE, OXAGON, and SINDALAH are all powered by clean energy.

name: Saudi Arabian Oil Company (Aramco)
description: World's largest energy company, actively transitioning into renewables. Partnered with major solar developers and investing in solar-powered facilities to reduce carbon emissions under its sustainability roadmap.

name: SABIC (Saudi Basic Industries Corporation)
description: One of the world's largest petrochemicals manufacturers, producing chemicals, polymers, fertilizers, metals and specialties. Majority-owned by Saudi Aramco.

name: SIPCHEM (Saudi International Petrochemical Company)
description: Produces methanol, acetic acid, vinyl acetate and other chemical products. Listed on Tadawul (Saudi Stock Exchange).

name: TASNEE (National Industrialization Company)
description: Largest private sector industrial company in Saudi Arabia. Produces TiO₂, petrochemicals, metals, and packaging materials.

name: SAFCO (Saudi Arabian Fertilizers Company)
description: One of the world's largest producers of urea and ammonia-based fertilizers. Subsidiary of SABIC.
